description: Embral reward system — ages 4-8, neurodivergent-first, family-centered educational RPG
design_principles: No punishment for failure — rewards effort and try-again, not perfection
No real-money purchases — everything earned through play, learning, and family time
Local-only multiplayer — competition is WITH family, never against strangers online
Streaks never break — sick days and missed days never erase progress
Family bonding is mechanically rewarded — showing up together IS the game
Hardware expands depth — base game complete without any extra hardware

gold.name: Gold
gold.description: Earned from quests, exploration, and daily activities. Spent on cosmetics and house items.

elemental_essence.name: Elemental Essence
elemental_essence.description: Rare drops from dungeons and boss encounters. Used to evolve Wisps.

play_points.name: Play Points
play_points.description: Earned from local mini-games. Ezra vs companion or family members (up to 4 players). Spent at the Play Shop for exclusive items.
play_points.notes: Participation always earns some. Winning earns more. Never zero for trying.

raid_points.name: Raid Points
raid_points.description: Earned from nightly review sessions with a parent. Collecting 3 in one week unlocks the Weekend Family Raid.

nightly_review.description: Josh and Ezra review the day (or week) together. Short, structured, bonding-focused. Not a test.
nightly_review.trigger: Parent opens the Review portal from Hearthveil. Evening or bedtime.

check_in_questions: What was one thing you felt proud of today?
Was there anything that felt hard or tricky?
Did you help someone or did someone help you?
What's one thing you want to try or learn tomorrow?
format.notes: Questions are shown visually with simple icons. Ezra can tap an answer or speak one. No wrong answers. Parent guides the conversation.
format.duration: 5-10 minutes. Short enough to be sustainable nightly.

raid_point_thresholds.description: Ezra earns a Raid Point when the nightly review hits a meaningful moment. These are qualitative, parent-judged, not automated.
thresholds: Ezra articulated a feeling without prompting
Ezra identified a moment of persistence or trying again
Ezra mentioned helping someone or being kind
Ezra expressed curiosity about something new
Ezra acknowledged something hard without shutting down
Parent and Ezra shared a genuine laugh or moment of connection
raid_point_thresholds.parent_action: Parent taps the Raid Point orb when a threshold moment happens. It's a judgment call — there's no automated tracking of these.
raid_point_thresholds.raid_point_per_session: Max 1 raid point per nightly review session.
raid_point_thresholds.weekly_reset: Monday. Raid points reset whether or not the Family Raid was triggered.

weekly_review.description: Optional longer version on Sunday night covering the whole week.
weekly_review.bonus: Weekly review session awards 2 Raid Points if completed, regardless of threshold moments.
weekly_review.format: Same questions but Ezra looks back at the week's quests and achievements in-game together with parent.

weekend_family_raid.description: Triggered when Ezra earns 3+ Raid Points in a week. Saturday or Sunday — family chooses when.
weekend_family_raid.philosophy: The Raid is a real-world shared activity framed as an epic game event. Ignavarr narrates it.
weekend_family_raid.unlock_notification: Friday evening: Ignavarr sends a message — 'A great challenge stirs. Return this weekend to face it together.'
weekend_family_raid.what_it_is: A special co-op family quest that is different every week. Drawn from a rotating pool of activity types. Always involves at least one parent participating alongside Ezra.

knowledge_raid.name: The Scholar's Challenge
knowledge_raid.format: Trivia or learning game. Parent and Ezra on the same team vs Ignavarr's questions.
knowledge_raid.example: Ignavarr asks 10 questions mixing what Ezra learned this week with fun family trivia.
knowledge_raid.educational_tie: Reinforces the week's quest themes through review disguised as challenge.

physical_raid.name: The Forge Trial
physical_raid.format: Physical activity challenge. Balance board, movement, or obstacle course.
physical_raid.example: Complete a real-world obstacle course together. Wii board tracks balance. PS5 tracks motion.
physical_raid.hardware_fallback: If no hardware: simple floor-is-lava or dance challenge. Same fun.

creative_raid.name: The Drift Gauntlet
creative_raid.format: Creative challenge. Drawing, building, storytelling, music.
creative_raid.example: Build the tallest tower from household items. Or draw a scene from Ezra's favorite quest this week.

card_game_raid.name: The Vault Duel
card_game_raid.format: A real physical card game played together. Can use Embral-themed printable cards.
card_game_raid.example: Simple matching game, Go Fish with Embral creature cards, or a custom Wisp card battle.
card_game_raid.educational_tie: Number recognition, pattern matching, turn-taking, strategic thinking.

cooking_raid.name: The Rootstead Feast
cooking_raid.format: Cook or prepare something together as a family.
cooking_raid.example: Make a healthy snack. Ezra measures, pours, mixes. Parent supervises.
cooking_raid.educational_tie: Life skills, math (measuring), nutrition, following directions.

story_raid.name: The Tidemark Epic
story_raid.format: Collaborative storytelling. Parent and Ezra build a story together with Ignavarr as narrator.
story_raid.example: Ignavarr starts a story. Ezra and parent take turns adding sentences. Ends with an illustrated page Ezra can keep.

nature_raid.name: The Spire Expedition
nature_raid.format: Outdoor observation challenge. Only works with good weather — indoor fallback always available.
nature_raid.example: Find 5 different types of plants, 3 insects, count birds. Document in an adventure journal.

failure_acceptance_raid.name: The Drift's Falling Challenge
failure_acceptance_raid.format: A deliberately hard challenge designed to be failed and tried again. Multiple attempts encouraged.
failure_acceptance_raid.example: Build the tallest block tower possible. It WILL fall. Try again. Who can rebuild fastest after a fall?
failure_acceptance_raid.educational_tie: Growth mindset, persistence, flexible thinking, frustration tolerance.
failure_acceptance_raid.notes: This one is special — the FALL is the point. Ignavarr has special dialogue for when it falls: 'MAGNIFICENT failure! Do it again!'

boss_mechanic.description: At the climax of the Family Raid, Ignavarr presents the Raid Boss encounter.
boss_mechanic.format: A final challenge tied to the raid type. Completing it = defeat the boss.
examples: Answer the 3 hardest review questions correctly
Complete the obstacle course one more time after failing
Finish the story with a satisfying ending
boss_mechanic.failure_handling: Boss can be attempted unlimited times. No penalty for failing. 'We try again together.'

on_completion.family_raid_shop_duration: Until next Monday — shop closes if not used

family_raid_shop.description: Exclusive shop only accessible after completing a Family Raid. Items rotate. Cannot be accessed any other way.

items.name: Ignavarr's Ember
items.description: A tiny flame that floats in Ezra's room in-game

items.name: Family Crest Banner
items.description: Customizable banner for Player's House. Grows as more raids are won.

items.name: Raid Wisp Egg
items.description: Random rare Wisp egg. Cannot be found any other way.

items.name: Bond Ribbon
items.description: Evolves a companion with max bond. Rare.

items.name: Memory Crystal
items.description: Records a raid achievement. Adds to the Trophy Room in Player's House.

items.name: Companion Outfit Unlock
items.description: Exclusive outfit only from raid shop.

items.name: Realm Landmark Unlock
items.description: Unlocks a hidden area in one realm — changes each raid.

family_raid_shop.rotation: 3 items available per raid. Different each time. Some items are rarer than others.
family_raid_shop.raid_exclusive_marker: All items have a small Family Crest icon showing they came from a Family Raid

mini_games.description: Local multiplayer mini-games. Ezra vs Companion (AI-controlled) or up to 4 local human players.
mini_games.philosophy: Mini-games are optional, always fun, and double as educational reinforcement when tied to quests.

player_modes.solo_vs_companion: Ezra plays against their AI companion
player_modes.two_player: Ezra + one family member, same screen

educational_tie.academic_mini_games: Some mini-games are tied to active quests — completing them counts as quest progress
educational_tie.active_mini_games: Physical mini-games (Wii) count toward Forge Run realm progress

play_points_earned.notes: Participation minimum ensures no one goes home empty-handed

sample_mini_games.name: Wisp Match
sample_mini_games.description: Memory matching game with Wisp cards. Educational: pattern recognition, memory.

sample_mini_games.name: Word Catch
sample_mini_games.description: Words fall from the sky. Catch the ones that rhyme with the target word.

sample_mini_games.name: Balance Duel
sample_mini_games.description: Both players balance on Wii board. Whoever holds balance longest wins.
sample_mini_games.hardware_fallback: Floor balance challenge — stand on one foot

sample_mini_games.name: Emotion Sort
sample_mini_games.description: Faces appear. Sort them into feeling buckets before time runs out.

sample_mini_games.name: Number Race
sample_mini_games.description: First to correctly tap the number that answers the equation wins the round.

gym_battles.description: One per realm. Major milestone encounters. Primarily hardware-powered for physical engagement. Base fallback always available.
gym_battles.unlock: Complete all 3 starter NPCs in a realm
gym_battles.philosophy: Gym battles are the physical celebration of mastering a realm. They should feel epic.

hardware_integration.wii_balance_board: Balance-based challenges — lean to dodge, hold steady to charge power
hardware_integration.wii_wiimote: Swing/point mechanics — aim attacks, swipe to dodge
hardware_integration.wii_nunchuck: Movement — walk character through gym arena
hardware_integration.ps5_dualsense: Haptic feedback for hits and blocks, motion for dodge rolls
hardware_integration.base_fallback: Keyboard/mouse turn-based version of same encounter — same content, different input

ember_hollow_gym.name: The Heart Trial
ember_hollow_gym.mechanic: Identify and match emotions correctly to weaken boss. Physical: balance board sways reflect emotional state.
ember_hollow_gym.educational_tie: Emotion regulation and identification under pressure

tidemark_gym.name: The Word Surge
tidemark_gym.mechanic: Spell words correctly to build a bridge. Physical: Wiimote swings to 'throw' letter blocks.
tidemark_gym.educational_tie: Spelling and phonics under timed challenge

forge_run_gym.name: The Forge Gauntlet
forge_run_gym.mechanic: Full physical obstacle — balance board + wiimote + nunchuck combined. Base: precision platformer section.
forge_run_gym.educational_tie: Gross motor integration, coordination, spatial awareness
forge_run_gym.notes: This is THE hardware showcase gym. Most physical gym in the game.

rootstead_gym.name: The Life Lodge Trial
rootstead_gym.mechanic: Complete a multi-step life skill sequence correctly and quickly. Physical: gesture-based actions.

the_spire_gym.name: The Vault Challenge
the_spire_gym.mechanic: Solve logic/math puzzles while the boss applies pressure. Timed. Physical: PS5 haptic pulses on correct answers.
the_spire_gym.educational_tie: Math and logic under mild challenge

the_drift_gym.name: The Creation Crucible
the_drift_gym.mechanic: Creative challenge — draw or build something matching a prompt before timer runs. Physical: motion drawing with wiimote.

first_clear.badge_display: Shown in Player's House trophy room
first_clear.special_item: Unique item per gym — cosmetic or wisp accessory

end_of_dungeon_boss.description: Every dungeon ends with a boss encounter. Harder than regular Wisp battles. Multi-phase.
end_of_dungeon_boss.mechanic: Phase 1: Standard battle. Phase 2: Environmental change adds a puzzle layer.

mini_bosses.description: Pop-up encounters throughout the dungeon. Stronger than standard Wisps, weaker than end boss.

milestones.25: Ground mounts unlocked — companion home slot +1
milestones.50: New content layer — companion home slot +1
milestones.75: New content layer — companion home slot +1
milestones.100: Ascension — flying mounts unlocked, new Ignavarr story chapter

npc_mastery.how_it_works: Score recorded per quest (0.0-1.0). Average of last 5 above 0.8 = mastery level up.

level_benefits.3: NPC shares personal lore and bonus dialogue
level_benefits.5: Mastery reward — unique item or pet tied to that NPC

play_shop.description: Exclusive items only available with Play Points from mini-games.

daily_login.philosophy: Rewards showing up. No streaks that break. Sick days never punished.
daily_login.reset: Resets to day 1 after day 7. No streak-breaking. No penalty for gaps.

no_these_things.no_season_passes: No paid battle passes. No FOMO mechanics.
no_these_things.no_master_ball: No catch-rate bypasses — earning a capture is part of the game.
no_these_things.no_online_pvp: No competition against strangers. Local family only.
no_these_things.no_real_money: Cannot purchase gold, essence, or any advantage with real money.
